summary: Consolidate 9 more copy-pasted/near-duplicate test builders (unit/member/kind-facts/findings/satisfies/requirement helpers) and fold check_harness into check_in

edit.description: Extend further (chain continues from the recordwriters slice, now shipped). Add `pub fn surface_unit(member: &Member, member_doc: &str, dir: &Path) -> Unit` (tests/command_kind.rs:48, tests/agent_kind.rs:47, identical); `pub fn skill_member(name: &str, description: &str, body: &str) -> PayloadMember` (tests/lock_declaration_rows.rs:80, tests/emit.rs:87, identical); `pub fn rule_member(name: &str, paths: Option<&[&str]>, body: &str) -> PayloadMember` — the Option-taking superset (tests/emit.rs:73 today); tests/lock_declaration_rows.rs:93's non-Option 3-arg callers pass `Some(paths)`; `pub fn rule_kind_facts(...) -> KindFactRow` and `pub fn skill_kind_facts(...) -> KindFactRow`, each parameterized by the provider/registration values the 2-3 copies diverge on (tests/lock_declaration_rows.rs:52,35; tests/emit.rs:47,60; tests/acceptance.rs:114); `pub fn skill_surface_unit(skill: &Member, name: Option<&str>) -> Unit` — tests/acceptance.rs:50's no-name 1-arg callers pass `None` and keep deriving from `skill.id`, tests/extract_equivalence.rs:40's name-carrying callers pass `Some(name)`; `pub fn findings_for<'a>(findings: &'a [String], rule: &str) -> Vec<&'a String>` (tests/memory_gate.rs:77, tests/gate_fail_loud.rs:62, tests/coverage_note.rs:106, identical); `pub fn author_rule_satisfies(root: &Path, name: &str, requirements: &[&str])` (tests/coverage.rs:252, tests/requirement_roster.rs:35, identical); `pub fn requirement(name: &str, required: bool, kind: Option<&str>) -> RequirementRow` — the union of three shapes (tests/gate_fail_loud.rs:36 passes `false, None`; tests/coverage.rs:39 passes `required, None`; tests/requirement_roster.rs:111 passes `false, Some(kind)`). Fold `check_harness`'s `(Vec<String>, bool)` shape (tests/coverage_note.rs:87) and its `Vec<String>` shape (tests/memory_gate.rs:59) into the existing `check_in`/`CheckRun` family (tests/common/mod.rs:114-134, already added by TEST-FIXTURE-HELPERS-CONSOLIDATE) — same job (run `temper check --reporter github`, parse `::`-prefixed lines), not a third near-duplicate helper.

Delete local `skill_member` (line 80), `rule_member` (line 93, 3-arg `&[&str]` shape — pass `Some(paths)`), `rule_kind_facts` (line 52), `skill_kind_facts` (line 35); call sites use `common::*`, each supplying its own provider/registration values.
Delete local `skill_member` (line 87), `rule_member` (line 73, already `Option`-shaped), `rule_kind_facts` (line 47), `skill_kind_facts` (line 60); call sites use `common::*`.
Delete local `skill_kind_facts` (line 114), `skill_surface_unit` (line 50, no-name 1-arg shape); call sites use `common::*`, passing `None` for the name param.
Delete local `skill_surface_unit` (line 40, 2-arg name-carrying shape); call sites use `common::skill_surface_unit`, passing `Some(name)`.

Delete local `findings_for` (line 77) and local `check_harness` (line 59, `Vec<String>` shape); call sites use `common::findings_for` and the shared `check_in`/`CheckRun` family, adapting to its return shape.
Delete local `findings_for` (line 106) and local `check_harness` (line 87, `(Vec<String>, bool)` shape); call sites use `common::findings_for` and the shared `check_in`/`CheckRun` family.
Delete local `findings_for` (line 62) and local `requirement` (line 36, 1-arg shape — pass `false, None`); call sites use `common::*`.
Delete local `author_rule_satisfies` (line 252) and local `requirement` (line 39, 2-arg `required`-carrying shape — pass `required, None`); call sites use `common::*`.
Delete local `author_rule_satisfies` (line 35) and local `requirement` (line 111, 2-arg `kind`-carrying shape — pass `false, Some(kind)`); call sites use `common::*`.

tests.asserts: lock-row fixtures built from skill_member/rule_member/rule_kind_facts/skill_kind_facts are unchanged after switching to the shared, parameterized common helpers
author_rule_satisfies-driven and requirement()-driven roster scenarios are unchanged for every (required, kind) combination in use today
check_harness's success-bool-carrying scenarios are unchanged after folding into the shared check_in/CheckRun family

acceptance: tests/common exports one surface_unit, skill_member, rule_member, rule_kind_facts, skill_kind_facts, skill_surface_unit, findings_for, author_rule_satisfies, requirement (each parameterized to its callers' full range), and check_harness folds into the existing check_in/CheckRun family; no duplicate or near-duplicate definition of any of these remains in the 11 edited test files; `cargo test` green.
notes: Found in job 4's residue sweep at ef72da3, second half of the pair filed with TEST-HELPER-DUPES-CONSOLIDATE(recordwriters). Blocker shipped at ec2dd42 (6-builder slice) + ff09cee (ship commit) — unblocked this tick; all 9 cited duplicates re-verified present at ff09cee with line numbers refreshed (ec2dd42 shifted every one). check_harness (memory_gate.rs, coverage_note.rs) still folds into check_in/CheckRun rather than becoming a third near-duplicate. scoped at ff09cee

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper`, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`, now at 0.0.5 pending publish — SDK-VERSION-LOCKSTEP); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. Re-verified at ff09cee: still no `.github/workflows/release.yml` (only `temper.yml`, a check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest (`temper-flume-harness`).

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (distribution.md).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, distribution.md). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (distribution.md 'Fail-loud invariant').
notes: Release infra outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm `@dtmd/temper`; ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs range-correctness shipped in SCAFFOLD-OUTPUT-VALID (^0.0.4); release owns only future re-pins.
